data.title: Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson Was Making $150K In WWE When He Asked 'Who's The Highest Paid?' — He Wanted Double Steve Austin, But Ended Up Getting $15M
data.description:
Dwayne “The Rock” Johnson is a global box-office force today, but his legendary hustle started with a much leaner paycheck. 

Long before he was building a tequila empire or headlining stadium-sized business ventures, he was grinding through the WWE on a gritty $150,000 contract—a figure that barely covered the grueling reality of 300 days on the road, paying for his own hotels, rental cars, and late-night diner meals out of his own pocket.

He shared that story during an appearance on the "PBD Podcast" in 2024 with host Patrick Bet-David, looking back at the moment his career and financial outlook shifted.

Don't Miss:

A $2 Million Ask That Reset The Conversation

By the late 1990s, Johnson had become one of WWE's top stars during its boom period. When it came time to renegotiate, he went straight to WWE chief Vince McMahon and kept it simple.

"Who is the highest paid on the roster?" he recalled saying on the podcast.

After learning that Stone Cold Steve Austin was earning $1 million, Johnson aimed higher and asked for $2 million, positioning himself not just on current status but where he believed he was headed.

Instead of agreeing, McMahon pushed him to understand the business behind the numbers, from costs and overhead to how revenue was actually generated.

The Moment That Changed The Number

Johnson took that seriously and quickly immersed himself in the financial side of WWE. When he returned, his approach had shifted, and he asked to match Austin's $1 million instead.

Trending: Skip the Regrets: The Essential Retirement Tips Experts Wish Everyone Knew Earlier.

That's when the conversation turned.

"Now that you've taken yourself through that and understand the business… you're going to make $15 million this year," Johnson said McMahon told him.

The figure wasn't just a guarantee. It reflected total projected earnings, including pay-per-view bonuses, merchandise, and live event revenue during WWE's surge at the time.

Bet-David reacted in real time to the jump.

"That's $150K a year to $15 million," he said. "That was crazy."

Johnson's response summed up the moment.

"Are you kidding me?!" he said.

The Same Mindset, Bigger Results

That experience reframed how Johnson approached money. It shifted the focus from base salary to total upside and participation in growth.

Years later, that same thinking showed up in his business ventures. His tequila brand Teremana, launched in 2020, quickly became one of the fastest-growing spirits brands in the U.S.

According to Forbes, the brand shipped 300,000 cases in its first year, the largest launch in spirits history. By 2023, it surpassed 1 million cases annually.

Industry estimates reported its valuation at about $3.5 billion, with expansion into more than 20 international markets.

The throughline is clear. A $150,000 starting point, a $2 million ask, and a deeper understanding of the business turned into a $15 million year and a long-term blueprint that still shows up decades later.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Cancer Drug Developer Nanobiotix Denies Any Active Takeover Process
data.description:
The stock's movement follows a statement clarifying that there is no ongoing process or assessment for a takeover, which has helped to stabilize investor sentiment as broader markets are experiencing gains.

NBTX Denies Takeover Talks Amid Reports

Additionally, Nanobiotix is a late-stage clinical biotechnology firm that aims to revolutionize cancer treatment through its proprietary nanotechnology.

This focus on enhancing treatment outcomes for patients is critical as the company continues to develop its product candidates.

In 2023, the companies inked a global licensing, co-development, and commercialization agreement for the investigational, potential first-in-class radioenhancer NBTXR3.

In 2025, Johnson & Johnson amended the agreement safeguarding Nanobiotix's path to sustainable cash flow through significant potential milestone payments.

The shift significantly reduces Nanobiotix's financial burden and allows the company to focus on advancing its clinical pipeline.

Nanobiotix Technical Analysis

Currently, the stock is trading 2.4% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) but is 18.5% above its 50-day SMA, demonstrating longer-term strength.

Over the past 12 months, shares have skyrocketed by 774.83%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 47.95, which is considered neutral territory. Meanwhile, MACD is at 1.0828, below its signal line at 1.9719, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $32.50
  • Key Support: $30.00

Nanobiotix Benzinga Edge Momentum Score 99.58

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Nanobiotix S.A. American Depositary Shares, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 99.58) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Nanobiotix's Benzinga Edge signal reveals a momentum-driven story, indicating strong performance relative to the market.

NBTX Price Action: Nanobiotix shares were up 6.17% at $31.07 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: What's Behind The Jump In Kodiak AI Shares?
data.description:
• Kodiak AI shares are climbing with conviction. Why is KDK stock surging?

Autonomous Vehicle To Deter Drone Swarms

Kodiak AI, in partnership with Epirus and General Dynamics Land Systems, unveiled the Leonidas Autonomous Ground Vehicle (AGV). The AGV integrates Epirus’ high-power microwave platform onto a commercial-grade truck equipped with Kodiak’s AI-powered autonomous driving system.

Being autonomous, the vehicle can operate without human intervention. It can also be teleoperated to extend the counter-UAS line of defense across fixed-site and expeditionary mission sets. This advanced defense system potentially offers a new layer of autonomous drone defense for critical assets and infrastructure.

KDK Stock Stays Below Key Averages

KDK is trading 7.8% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 9.3% below its 100-day SMA, keeping the stock in a technically weak posture despite today's bounce. Shares are down 3.40% over the past 12 months and, within the $5.43 to $11.35 52-week range, the stock is positioned closer to its lows than its highs.

Momentum gauges are still cautious: RSI is at 32.27, which sits in neutral territory but is getting close to oversold conditions. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.2982 and remains below its signal line at -0.1704, a bearish configuration that suggests rallies may still face selling pressure.

The combination of RSI in the 30–50 range with bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $8.50
  • Key Support: $7.50

KDK Shares Trade Higher Wednesday

KDK Price Action: Kodiak shares are up 4.71% at $7.67 at the time of publication on Wednesday. The stock is trading 32.4% below its 52-week high. Today's trading range spans from a low of $7.55 to a high of $8.00.

Image: Shutterstock
